title: arenaria ciliata subsp. multicaulis
intro: Arenaria ciliata subsp. multicaulis, also known as the sea pink or sea campion, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, characterized by its pink or white flowers and multiple stems. Traditionally, Arenaria ciliata subsp. multicaulis has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and protect against microbial infections. In herbal preparations, Arenaria ciliata subsp. multicaulis is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
